name: llama-vscode-fim
description: Local LLM-assisted text completion using llama.cpp

llama-vscode-fim.launch_completion.description: Shell command for starting local fim llama.cpp server

llama-vscode-fim.launch_training_complet.description: Shell command for starting training a completion (fim) model from the menu

llama-vscode-fim.lora_completion.description: Path to the lora adapter file for the completion model. If not empty it will be used (appends --lora lora_completion) on starting the completion server with launch_completion

llama-vscode-fim.endpoint.description: The URL to be used by the extension for code completion.

llama-vscode-fim.max_parallel_completion.description: The max number of parallel completions. Switching between completions could be done with Alt+] (next) or Alt =+[ (previous).

llama-vscode-fim.new_completion_model_po.description: The port will be used when a new completion model is created.

llama-vscode-fim.new_completion_model_ho.description: The host will be used when a new completion model is created.

name.description: Name for this model to be shown to the user

endpoint.description: The endpoint, from where to access the model

aiModel.description: The name of the AI model as expected by the provider

isKeyRequired.description: Is key requried for the endpoint

localStartCommand.description: Command to be used for sterting the model locally.

llama-vscode-fim.completion_models_list.description: The list of the completion (FIM) models, which could be selected

name.description: Name for this model to be shown to the user

endpoint.description: The endpoint, from where to access the model

aiModel.description: The name of the AI model as expected by the provider

isKeyRequired.description: Is key requried for the endpoint

localStartCommand.description: Command to be used for sterting the model locally.

envStartLastUsed.description: If true - starts the last used env on startup.

default.name: Local, full package - min, gpt-oss 20B ( > 24GB VRAM | HD: 16 GB)
Empty - no models
default.description: For cases when the settings (endpoint*, Launch_*, Api_key*,  Ai_model) are used for configuring which servers to be used by llama-vscode-fim instead of env.

llama-vscode-fim.ask_completion_llamacpp.description: The list of envs, which could be selected

llama-vscode-fim.env_start_last_used.description: If true - starts the last used env on startup.

llama-vscode-fim.ask_install_llamacpp.description: If true, on starting VS Code - installation of llama.cpp will be suggested.

llama-vscode-fim.ask_upgrade_llamacpp_ho.description: How offen to ask the user to upgrade llama.cpp in hours.

llama-vscode-fim.env_start_last_used_con.description: If true, before starting the last used env, the user is asked for confirmation. Used only if env_start_last_used = true

llama-vscode-fim.auto.description: If code completion should be triggered automatically (true) or only by pressing Ctrl+l.

llama-vscode-fim.debounce_ms.description: Milliseconds to wait after the last keystroke before sending a completion request (0 = disabled). Useful on low-end hardware to avoid triggering inference on every keystroke.

llama-vscode-fim.health_check_interval_s.description: Models health check interval in seconds

llama-vscode-fim.health_check_compl_enab.description: Works only for llama.cpp servers - enables health check for completion model

llama-vscode-fim.n_prefix.description: number of lines before the cursor location to include in the local prefix

llama-vscode-fim.n_suffix.description: number of lines after  the cursor location to include in the local suffix

llama-vscode-fim.n_predict.description: max number of tokens to predict

llama-vscode-fim.t_max_prompt_ms.description: max alloted time for the prompt processing (TODO: not yet supported)

llama-vscode-fim.t_max_predict_ms.description: max alloted time for the prediction

llama-vscode-fim.show_info.description: show extra info about the inference (false - disabled, true - show extra info in status line)

llama-vscode-fim.max_line_suffix.description: do not auto-trigger FIM completion if there are more than this number of characters to the right of the cursor

llama-vscode-fim.max_cache_keys.description: max number of cached completions to keep in result_cache

llama-vscode-fim.ring_n_chunks.description: max number of chunks to pass as extra context to the server (0 to disable)

llama-vscode-fim.ring_chunk_size.description: max size of the chunks (in number of lines). Note: adjust these numbers so that you don't overrun your context at ring_n_chunks = 64 and ring_chunk_size = 64 you need ~32k context

llama-vscode-fim.ring_scope.description: the range around the cursor position (in number of lines) for gathering chunks after FIM

llama-vscode-fim.ring_update_ms.description: how often to process queued chunks in normal mode

llama-vscode-fim.use_openai_endpoint.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] Use OAI endpoint. Slow and poor quality - avoid using

llama-vscode-fim.openai_client_model.description: The FIM friendly model supported by your OpenAI compatible endpoint to be used (e.g., Qwen2.5-Coder-14B-4-bit)

llama-vscode-fim.openai_prompt_template.description: The prompt template to be used for the OpenAI compatible endpoint.

llama-vscode-fim.debug_log_enabled.description: Enable or disable log output to the Output channel and log file.

enumDescriptions: DEBUG – very verbose, every completion request and cache hit
INFO  – model selection, activation, health checks
WARN  – non-fatal problems (missed endpoint, degraded health)
ERROR – failures that stop a feature from working
llama-vscode-fim.debug_log_level.description: Minimum log level written to the llama-vscode Output channel and log file.

llama-vscode-fim.debug_log_to_file.description: Write log entries to a file in the extension log directory (shown in the Output channel on activation).
